all.en: My favorite baseball team reached the championship final after a long, difficult season.
all.zh: 我最喜愛的棒球隊在漫長而艱辛的賽季後闖進了總冠軍賽。

all.en: My father surprised me with two tickets to the final game as a birthday present.
all.zh: 父親給了我一份生日驚喜，那就是兩張總冠軍賽的門票。

all.en: The stadium was completely packed with over forty thousand cheering fans.
all.zh: 球場裡擠滿了超過四萬名歡呼的球迷。

all.en: Everyone was wearing team jerseys, waving flags and singing traditional cheering songs.
all.zh: 每個人都穿著球隊球衣、揮舞旗幟並唱著傳統的應援歌曲。

all.en: The atmosphere felt electric, and I could feel the excitement vibrating through my whole body.
all.zh: 現場氣氛電力十足，我能感受到那份興奮在我全身震動。

all.en: In the first inning, our pitcher struck out three batters in a row.
all.zh: 在第一局，我們的投手連續三振了三名打者。

all.en: The crowd roared so loudly that I could not hear my father's voice beside me.
all.zh: 觀眾的吼聲如此震耳欲聾，連在我身旁的父親說話聲都聽不見。

all.en: In the middle of the game, we shared hot dogs, popcorn and ice-cold drinks.
all.zh: 比賽中段，我們分享著熱狗、爆米花和冰涼的飲料。

all.en: By the ninth inning, the score remained tied, and tension filled every corner of the stadium.
all.zh: 到了第九局，比分仍然平手，緊張的氣氛瀰漫到球場的每個角落。

all.en: With two outs, our cleanup hitter sent the ball flying over the outfield wall.
all.zh: 在兩出局的情況下，我們的第四棒打者把球轟出了外野牆。

all.en: Strangers around us hugged each other and cried tears of pure happiness.
all.zh: 我們周圍的陌生人互相擁抱，流下了純粹喜悅的淚水。

all.en: Watching that championship game live with my father is a memory I will always treasure.
all.zh: 和父親一起現場觀看那場總冠軍賽，是一段我將永遠珍藏的回憶。

all.en: After the final out, my father and I joined the crowd singing the victory anthem.
all.zh: 最後一個出局之後，我和父親加入人群一同唱起勝利之歌。

all.en: We waited nearly an hour to leave the stadium because the exits were packed.
all.zh: 我們等了將近一小時才離開球場，因為出口擠滿了人。

all.en: Outside, fans were dancing in the streets and honking car horns in celebration.
all.zh: 場外球迷在街上跳舞，並按著喇叭歡慶勝利。

all.en: My father bought me a championship cap and a poster signed by the team.
all.zh: 父親幫我買了一頂冠軍紀念帽和一張球隊簽名的海報。

all.en: On the train home, we replayed every key moment of the game in detail.
all.zh: 在回家的火車上，我們把比賽的每個關鍵時刻都仔細回味了一遍。

all.en: My mother greeted us at the door with a huge cake decorated in team colors.
all.zh: 母親在門口迎接我們，端著一個以球隊顏色裝飾的大蛋糕。

all.en: The next day, news headlines praised our team's incredible comeback against all odds.
all.zh: 隔天的新聞頭條讚揚我們的球隊在劣勢中完成驚人逆轉。

all.en: I posted a photo of my ticket online, and old classmates instantly responded.
all.zh: 我把票根的照片發到網路上，老同學們立刻紛紛留言。

all.en: My father said he had not felt that proud and emotional in many years.
all.zh: 父親說他已經很多年沒有感受到那樣的驕傲與激動了。

all.en: From that day on, baseball became our special bond that nothing could replace.
all.zh: 從那天起，棒球成了我們之間獨特而無可取代的連結。

all.en: A month later, the team invited season ticket holders to a special meet and greet.
all.zh: 一個月後，球隊邀請了季票持有者參加一場特別的見面會。

all.en: My father and I waited in line for two hours to shake hands with the players.
all.zh: 我和父親排了兩個小時的隊，只為了和球員們握手。

all.en: The cleanup hitter remembered the championship moment and signed my poster personally.
all.zh: 那位第四棒打者還記得冠軍賽的瞬間，並親自為我的海報簽名。

all.en: He told us his home run was the proudest moment of his entire career.
all.zh: 他告訴我們，那支全壘打是他整個職業生涯中最自豪的時刻。

all.en: My father quietly wiped a tear from his eye while listening to the story.
all.zh: 父親在聆聽故事時，悄悄拭去眼角的一滴淚。

all.en: On the way home, we stopped by the stadium one more time to take photos.
all.zh: 回家路上，我們又繞到球場一次拍照留念。

all.en: My father decided to frame the signed poster and hang it in our living room.
all.zh: 父親決定把簽名海報裱框，掛在我們的客廳裡。

all.en: Now whenever guests visit, the conversation always begins with that legendary championship game.
all.zh: 現在每當客人來訪，話題總是從那場傳奇的冠軍賽開始。

all.en: My father promised to take me to the opening game of every season from now on.
all.zh: 父親承諾從今以後每年都帶我去看開幕戰。

all.en: Baseball was no longer just a sport but a tradition that belonged to us both.
all.zh: 棒球不再只是一項運動，而是屬於我們父子倆共同的傳統。

all.en: During spring training, my father and I drove three hours to watch practice games.
all.zh: 春訓期間，我和父親開了三小時的車去觀看練習賽。

all.en: The cleanup hitter spotted us in the front row and waved with a wide smile.
all.zh: 那位第四棒打者在前排看到我們，露出燦爛的微笑揮手致意。

all.en: He invited us into the dugout for a quick tour after the morning practice.
all.zh: 晨間練習結束後，他邀請我們進入休息區進行簡短的參觀。

all.en: My father was speechless as he stood on the field where champions had played.
all.zh: 父親站在冠軍曾征戰過的球場上，激動得說不出話來。

all.en: The coach overheard our story and gave me a baseball signed by the entire team.
all.zh: 教練聽到我們的故事，送了我一顆全隊簽名的棒球。

all.en: On the drive home, we listened to old broadcasts of legendary playoff games together.
all.zh: 回家的路上，我們一起聆聽傳奇季後賽的舊播報錄音。

all.en: My father said this trip had healed something deep inside his quiet heart.
all.zh: 父親說這趟旅行療癒了他內心深處某個沉默的角落。

all.en: That summer, we volunteered together at a youth baseball camp for underprivileged children.
all.zh: 那年夏天，我們一起在一個為弱勢兒童舉辦的青少年棒球營擔任志工。

all.en: My father coached pitching while I taught the basics of batting stance and timing.
all.zh: 父親教導投球，而我則教打擊站姿和揮棒時機的基礎。

all.en: Years later, the signed baseball still sits beside the framed poster in our living room.
all.zh: 多年後，那顆簽名棒球仍擺在我們客廳那張裱框海報的旁邊。
